pattern: Find the axis-aligned bounding box of all 1-cells: track the minimum and maximum row and column indices where a 1 appears, then return (max_row - min_row + 1) * (max_col - min_col + 1).
hints: Scan the entire grid to collect all (row, col) pairs where grid[row][col] == 1. The bounding rectangle is defined by min/max of these coordinates.
Area = (max_row - min_row + 1) * (max_col - min_col + 1). The problem guarantees at least one 1, so you never need to handle the empty case.
A single pass through the grid updating four variables (min_r, max_r, min_c, max_c) is sufficient — O(m*n) time and O(1) extra space.

edge_inputs.description: example 1: ones span full height and width
ones only on main diagonal of 3x3
single row, ones at both ends
single 1 in the middle of large grid

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, size)
    cols = rng.randint(1, size)
    # ensure at least one 1
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    # guarantee at least one 1
    r0 = rng.randint(0, rows - 1)
    c0 = rng.randint(0, cols - 1)
    grid[r0][c0] = 1
    return [grid]
